technical.lead: WSJ Apr 28 OpenAI-revenue-miss report broke the AI-trade tape
technical.body: Oracle -4%, AMD -3%, Broadcom -4%, SoftBank -10%; S&P -0.49% to 7,138.80, Nasdaq -0.9% to 24,663.80; FOMC Apr 29 (Powell's last) + MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META prints converge into a single 24h — — WSJ reports OpenAI — missed multiple monthly revenue targets — in early-2026 and fell short of the internal 1B-WAU year-end goal as Anthropic gained ground in coding/enterprise; CFO Sarah Friar warned compute-funding could tighten without re-acceleration. OpenAI rebutted ("ridiculous"). The Apr 28 selloff is the — first material crack in the "AI-capex on faith" tape — since Apr 17 and lands the hour before MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META post Q1 results today (Apr 29 after close) and Powell's final FOMC presser at 2:30 PM ET. Rates held at 3.50–3.75% (consensus 100%); aggregated 2026 Mag-7 AI capex ~$700B.********

citations.label: NBC News - Tech stocks slide following report on OpenAI missing key targets

technical.lead: CVE wave Apr 28: Milesight AIOT default-SSL-key (CVE-2026-32644, CVSS 9.8) + Totolink A8000RU triple (7240/7242/7243, all 9.8) + CISA KEV adds ConnectWise ScreenConnect CVE-2024-1708 & Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202 with May-12 federal deadline; SGLang CVE-2026-5760 still unpatched
technical.body: Apr 28 single-day publishes — four 9.8 RCEs — : Milesight cameras shipping with hard-coded SSL private keys, plus three more Totolink A8000RU OS-command-injection CVEs against firmware 7.1cu.643_b20200521 (extending the Apr 27-28 wave to 7 distinct CVEs in 72h). CISA's same-day KEV add includes the ConnectWise ScreenConnect path-traversal that has been chained with CVE-2024-1709 (auth bypass, CVSS 10.0) for 14+ months and the Windows Shell spoofing flaw exploited by APT28 since Dec-2025. SGLang's GGUF SSTI primitive (CVSS 9.8) — remains unpatched — more than 10 days post-disclosure.********

citations.label: CISA - Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog (Apr 28)

technical.body: Google TurboQuant 6x compression / 8x faster attention (ICLR 2026, Apr 23-25 Rio); SGLang PR #21419 lands 3-4 bit `--kv-cache-dtype turboquant`; PolyKV arxiv-paper Apr 27; community kvpress + KVTC + ChunkKV stacking — — TurboQuant's MSE-FWHT-rotation + 3.5-bit-Lloyd-Max method — matches FP16 on LongBench/Needle-in-Haystack — with no retraining; Google official Python impl due Q2 2026 but — community ports are already shipping — (MLX, Triton, vLLM/SGLang). SGLang PR #21419 wires `--kv-cache-dtype turboquant` first-class (initial impl had a hot-path dequant bug — fix #23134 hits 93-105% bf16 throughput). **PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27)** introduces a shared asymmetric KV-pool for multi-agent inference, int8 keys + 3-bit TurboQuant-MSE values via HF DynamicCache injection. NVIDIA `kvpress` reaches Decoding-Press; KVTC (20x) + PackKV (15.30x V) + TriAttention (10.7x) round out the stack.********

citations.label: Google Research - TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression

technical.lead: AI Dev 26 SF opens today (Apr 28-29) at Pier 48
technical.body: 3,000+ devs, Andrew Ng-hosted; Novita Sandbox launches Apr 28 to secure OpenClaw/Hermes/autonomous systems; Multica v0.2.18 (Apr 27); ClawHavoc poisoning campaign quantified at 1,184 malicious skills + Cisco's 36%-of-marketplace prompt-injection finding — — DeepLearning.AI's two-day flagship runs Pier 48 Mission Bay with tracks on — Agentic AI, Context Engineering, Multimodal AI Apps, AI Governance, Coding-with-AI — + AI Startup Track; speakers from Google DeepMind, Oracle, AMD, Arm, Neo4j, LangChain, LandingAI, Replit, AI21. Apr 28: — Novita AI launches a managed Sandbox — — managed NovitaClaw deployments, Sandbox Skills for OpenClaw, Hermes-compatible runtimes, persistent-node configs. **Multica v0.2.18** (Apr 27) extends the v0.2.16 Chat-V2 + right-click context-menus baseline. **ClawHavoc** quantified at **1,184 malicious skills** in ClawHub with self-erasing memory after install (Kurtz keynote, Antiy + Koi Security analysis).********

citations.label: PR Newswire - Novita AI Launches Sandbox to Secure OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and Autonomous Systems

technical.body: distill-corpus-into-skill-tree RAG that beats RAPTOR, dense retrieval, and agentic RAG on WixQA without an embedding index; "skills/harness" externalization wave continues with arxiv 2604.08224 (Apr 24) survey — — Corpus2Skill compiles a document corpus offline into a hierarchical skill directory; agent receives a bird's-eye view, drills into topic branches via progressively finer summaries, retrieves full docs by ID. — Highest quality on every WixQA metric — without vector DB at serve time. Pairs with Apr 24's — "Externalization in LLM Agents" — survey (arxiv 2604.08224) framing the move from weights → prompts → skills/harness/protocol infrastructure as the dominant 2026 design pattern — the same shift driving Anthropic's 17-skill repo, OpenClaw skills, AgentCore Managed Harness, and Multica's harness-orchestrator surface.********

citations.label: arXiv - Don't Retrieve, Navigate: Distilling Enterprise Knowledge into Navigable Agent Skills for QA and RAG (Corpus2Skill, 2604.14572)
